cards.title: Syntax Comparison
cards.question: How does Python syntax differ from Java syntax?
cards.answer: Python has a simpler and more readable syntax, allowing for fewer lines of code, while Java syntax is more complex and requires code to be enclosed within classes.

cards.title: Execution Model
cards.question: What is the difference between compilation in Java and interpretation in Python?
cards.answer: Python is an interpreted language which translates code at runtime, while Java compiles code into bytecode that runs on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM).

cards.title: Java Features
cards.question: What makes Java a robust programming language?
cards.answer: Java emphasizes reliability through strong memory management, exception handling, and type checking, which minimizes errors.
